x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces the Office of the Bronx Borough President's charter work (land-use/ULURP recommendations, community-board appointments, discretionary funding, newsroom, events) and adds apply_to_community_board — the net-new write workflow that today is a downloadable PDF or an email. Written to be templatable across all five Borough President offices.

server.name: io.nyc.bronxbp
server.title: Bronx Borough President
server.description: Agent-native access to the Office of the Bronx Borough President — land-use (ULURP) advisory recommendations, community-board appointments and discretionary funding awards (from NYC Open Data), plus the newsroom and public events, and the net-new ability to apply to serve on a Bronx community board. Only two of these entities are machine-readable today (Capital Funding mdgu-ar69, Bronx Community Boards wbau-xy7g); the rest are reverse-engineered from a Revize CMS brochure site with no API.
server.instructions: Use find_land_use_recommendations / get_land_use_recommendation to look up the Borough President's ULURP advisory dispositions (today published only as document-center PDFs). find_community_board_appointments lists who serves on the twelve Bronx community boards (open data). find_discretionary_funding answers 'what did the Borough President fund?' by fiscal year, community board, or council district (open data). find_press_releases / get_press_release cover the newsroom; find_events covers public events and meetings. apply_to_community_board (net-new) submits a resident's application to serve on a community board; it mutates state and needs an authenticated user — confirm the requested board, the applicant's name, and residence before submitting. list_my_community_board_applications reads the applicant's own submissions and status.

tools.name: find_land_use_recommendations
tools.title: Find Land-Use Recommendations
tools.description: Search the Borough President's ULURP / land-use advisory recommendations by community board, disposition, action type, date range, or free text.

tools.name: get_land_use_recommendation
tools.title: Get Land-Use Recommendation
tools.description: Fetch a single Borough President land-use recommendation by its ULURP/ZAP application number.

tools.name: find_community_board_appointments
tools.title: Find Community Board Appointments
tools.description: List appointments to the twelve Bronx community boards by board number, year, or recommending official. Backed by NYC Open Data (wbau-xy7g).

tools.name: find_discretionary_funding
tools.title: Find Discretionary Funding
tools.description: Search the Borough President's discretionary capital / expense funding awards by fiscal year, type, community board, or council district. Backed by NYC Open Data (mdgu-ar69).

tools.name: find_press_releases
tools.title: Find Newsroom Items
tools.description: List newsroom items (press releases, statements, letters, testimonies) by category, date range, or free text.

tools.name: get_press_release
tools.title: Get Newsroom Item
tools.description: Fetch a single newsroom item (release, statement, letter, or testimony) by id.

tools.name: find_events
tools.title: Find Events
tools.description: List the Borough President's public events and meetings by calendar (Events or Meetings) and date range.

tools.name: list_my_community_board_applications
tools.title: List My Community Board Applications
tools.description: List the authenticated applicant's own applications to serve on a Bronx community board, and their status — today there is no way to check this at all.

tools.name: apply_to_community_board
tools.title: Apply to a Community Board
tools.description: Submit a resident's application to serve on one of the Bronx's twelve community boards. NET-NEW workflow with no API today — currently a downloadable PDF or an email to the office. Requires an authenticated user and mutates state — confirm the requested board, the applicant's name, and Bronx residence before submitting.
